crypto._notes: Per-position cap for crypto signals (3.4.1). Wider spreads + 24/7 markets mean we size more conservatively than equity. Auto_trader detects crypto symbols via monitoring.crypto_adapter.is_crypto_symbol and uses this cap instead of auto_trade.max_position_usd. Set 0 to fall back to the default (500).

auto_trade._notes_stops: 6.1.1/6.1.2 — Initial-stop policy lives under top-level `stops` (see below), not here. Legacy `stop_loss_atr_multiple` is still honored when set for backward-compat with Phase 4.6 trend strategies.
auto_trade._notes: enabled=false → process_signals is never called. enabled=true + dry_run=true → logs would-be orders without submitting. enabled=true + dry_run=false → REAL Alpaca paper orders. Eligibility = (closed_outcomes >= min_outcomes) AND (mean_ret_pct >= min_mean_ret_pct) AND (sharpe_ish >= min_sharpe_ish). Strategies with `grace_period: true` in their TRACKED_STRATEGIES declaration bypass the min_outcomes gate while they accumulate data, and their max_position_usd is reduced by grace_period_size_multiplier (default 0.25 → quarter size). Once n >= min_outcomes the strategy graduates and normal thresholds apply. Cool-down: when a strategy's last cool_down_losers closed outcomes were ALL losers, pause new entries for cool_down_days trading days. Set cool_down_losers=0 to disable. Earnings veto: skip entries on a symbol within earnings_veto_days trading days of its next earnings event (populated by monitoring.earnings_calendar). Set to 0 to disable. Negative-sentiment veto: when veto_negative_sentiment=true, skip entries on symbols with >= negative_sentiment_threshold negative-sentiment news items in the last negative_sentiment_window_hours hours (requires news.sentiment populated by news_fetcher). Live-strategies routing: each strategy_id listed in live_strategies submits to a separate `alpaca_live` credentials section (paper=false). Empty list (default) keeps every strategy on paper. Adding a strategy here requires an alpaca_live section in credentials.json — without it, those signals SKIP_LIVE_CREDS_MISSING and never submit. Intraday master switch (5.2.3): intraday_enabled=false → auto_trader_intraday.process_intraday short-circuits with DISABLED_INTRADAY; intraday_fires still records signals (observe-only). intraday_enabled=true → intraday signals at each interval in intraday_intervals (default ['15m']) are processed through the same eligibility/sizing/regime pipeline as EOD signals.

stops._notes: 6.1.1 / 6.1.2 / 6.1.3 — Initial-stop policy. Stops at entry are computed as entry_price - (atr_multiplier × ATR_n) for longs (mirrored for shorts). Default ATR period is 14, default multiplier is 2.5. by_class.{strategy_class}.atr_multiplier lets a whole class override the default (6.1.2 sets mean_reversion → 2.0). per_strategy.{strategy_id}.atr_multiplier overrides everything else for a single strategy. fixed_percent_fallback (e.g. 0.05 = 5%) supplies a fallback stop when ATR can't be computed (<14 bars of history). 6.1.3 — set regime_aware=true to scale k by the current market regime: k_effective = k_base × regime_multipliers[current_regime]. Multiplier clamped to [0.7, 1.5] so a misconfigured table can't blow up the stop band. Setting any value to 0 / null at a level lets the next-level default win.

kelly._notes: 6.2.2 — Fractional Kelly sizing tier. Activated per-strategy by setting auto_trade.sizing_method='kelly_quarter'. fraction_of_kelly (default 0.25 = ¼ Kelly) is multiplied against the raw Kelly fraction; code hard-clamps this at 0.5 (½ Kelly) so config typos can never push to full Kelly. max_position_fraction (default 0.05 = 5%) caps any single position as a fraction of portfolio. min_samples (default 50) is the closed-outcome guard — below it, sizing falls back to the tiered (3.2.1) tier. Ross can promote a strategy to fraction_of_kelly=0.5 after it accumulates 200+ closed outcomes.

notes: These are non-secret settings. Edit freely.
